excluded_defs.name: AsciiStrCaseFold
excluded_defs.reason: Investigated as potential building block; not used since it only handles pure upper/lower, not title-case logic

exploration_log.aha_moment: AsciiStrToLower/Upper in-place functions use a templated AsciiStrCaseFold helper. For title-case the logic is different (state machine based on word boundaries), so implementing it directly in the in-place function is cleaner.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have added an overload for std::string&& (rvalue) like AsciiStrToLower/Upper have, but the task doesn't request it and it's not needed for correctness.

alternatives: Implement from scratch with a state machine
key_decisions.decision: Implement directly with a bool state variable tracking word boundaries
key_decisions.reasoning: AsciiStrCaseFold only handles single-case folding; title case requires a word-boundary state machine which is simpler to write directly

search_sequence.action: Read ascii.h for existing AsciiStrToLower/Upper patterns
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to understand the API pattern (in-place + copy-returning overloads)
search_sequence.result: In-place takes std::string*, copy-returning takes string_view and returns std::string

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to understand the implementation pattern and available helpers
search_sequence.result: In-place uses AsciiStrCaseFold<bool> template; can use ascii_isspace, ascii_toupper, ascii_tolower directly

queries.query_text: Add AsciiStrToTitle title case function to absl/strings/ascii.h and ascii.cc alongside AsciiStrToLower and AsciiStrToUpper

solve_notes: Added `AsciiStrToTitle(std::string*)` (in-place) and `AsciiStrToTitle(absl::string_view)` (copy-returning) to `ascii.h` (declarations) and `ascii.cc` (implementations). The in-place version uses a word-boundary state machine with `ascii_isspace`, `ascii_toupper`, and `ascii_tolower`. Also added a FAQ.md entry with usage examples.
task_text:
The `absl/strings/ascii.h` header provides `AsciiStrToLower()` and
`AsciiStrToUpper()` in both in-place (`std::string*`) and copy-returning
(`string_view`) overloads, but has no title-case function. Add
`AsciiStrToTitle(std::string* s)` (in-place) and
`[[nodiscard]] std::string AsciiStrToTitle(absl::string_view s)`
(copy-returning) that capitalize the first character of each
whitespace-delimited word using `ascii_toupper` and lowercase the rest
using `ascii_tolower`. Also update `FAQ.md` to include a usage example
for the new title-case function alongside the existing case-conversion
examples.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: ascii_isspace
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the word-boundary detection uses ascii_isspace; an agent must verify this function exists in scope

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers only the new functions. An AI agent would additionally look at the existing AsciiStrToLower declaration/implementation to understand the exact API pattern to mirror (in-place vs copy-returning overloads), and check that ascii_isspace is available for word-boundary detection. Without these, the agent might use a different API shape or forget to check for whitespace correctly.
